subject: Computer Networking
title: How Online Gaming Stays Fast
description: A short lesson to help you apply How Online Gaming Stays Fast.

hook.text: In an online game, 100 players act simultaneously. Everyone sees the same world in real time. How?

buildup.text: Games use UDP for speed. They send tiny position updates dozens of times per second to a central server.

discovery.text: The server reconciles everyone's actions into one truth and sends the result back to all players.

twist.text: Lag gives some players an unfair advantage. That's why competitive games invest heavily in low-latency servers.

climax.text: Prediction algorithms guess where you'll move next, smoothing the experience even when packets arrive late.

punchline.text: Online gaming is a latency war disguised as fun.

quiz.question: Why do online games typically use UDP instead of TCP?
options: UDP's lower overhead reduces latency for real-time updates
UDP is more reliable than TCP
